mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and core inflation (proxied by CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 203.5, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy. This sustained tightening compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 5.25% and CPIAUCSL at 203.7 both elevated
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS*CPIAUCSL interaction and forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration and rising recession risk. When monthly payroll growth turns negative or falls below 50,000 new jobs, equity investors reprice earnings expectations downward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month or growth < 50000 jobs
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following payroll declines are positive in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC between PAYEMS and forward returns remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) serve as a high-frequency proxy for consumer demand strength. When RSAFS growth accelerates above its 6-month moving average, it signals robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS surprises fail to produce positive returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) captures real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing health. When INDPRO declines month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, it signals stagflationary pressure that erodes profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 113.7063 and UNRATE at 4.7%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (INDPRO decline × UNRATE rise) shows Rank IC below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) lead consumer spending and construction employment by 2-3 months. When HOUST falls below 1,700 thousand units annualized, it signals weakening housing demand and forward-looking consumer retrenchment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1,795 thousand units, near trigger threshold
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following HOUST < 1700 are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC between HOUST and forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) serves as a real-time labor market health indicator. When UNRATE remains stable below 4.8% while PAYEMS growth remains positive, it signals tight labor markets and strong consumer income, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.8% AND PAYEMS growth > 100000 jobs month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.7% and PAYEMS at 135,500 thousand
regime_hint: tight labor market with full employment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (low UNRATE × strong PAYEMS) shows Rank IC below 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) declines by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals monetary policy easing and reduced discount rates on future corporate cash flows. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets front-run the easing cycle.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines > 0.5pp are negative in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS momentum and forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) represents the broadest measure of economic expansion. When GDPC1 growth decelerates below 1.5% annualized (measured quarter-over-quarter), it signals late-cycle slowdown and earnings compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 11,397.6 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following GDPC1 growth < 1.5% are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
